content: Architecture: Usyd-Mastery is a pure static (HTML/CSS/JS) multi-course study platform for University of Sydney CS courses. 4 hubs: 5270 (12ch), 5318 (6ch), Comp5046 (6ch), COMP9001 (5ch). All shared tool modules (quiz-hub, flashcards, progress-dashboard, knowledge-graph, mindmap, chat-panel, flashcard-srs, progress-tracker, study-base.css) live in root /shared/ with thin HTML wrappers per course using window.CONFIG pattern. localStorage keys follow comp{courseNum}_ prefix convention. sync-shared.sh propagates root shared files to hub copies.

Decision: localStorage key naming convention is comp{courseNumber}_{type} — types include: _lang, _progress, _flashcards, _chat_config, _quiz_missed_stack. Examples: comp5270_lang, comp5318_progress, comp5046_flashcards, comp9001_chat_config.

Decision: Shared tool page pattern — each tool (quiz-hub, flashcards, progress-dashboard, knowledge-graph) has shared CSS+JS in root /shared/ and thin HTML wrappers (~100-130 lines) in each hub's tools/ folder. HTML sets window.QUIZ_CONFIG / FLASHCARD_CONFIG / DASHBOARD_CONFIG / KG_CONFIG before loading the shared engine JS. study.css is split into /shared/study-base.css (1532 lines, structural) + per-hub theme files (~70 lines, CSS variables only).

User preference: User wants zero code redundancy — all shared logic must be in root /shared/, each course hub only contains thin config wrappers. When updating a feature (e.g., quiz UI), change root shared files only and it automatically applies to all courses. User communicates in English but rule says respond in Chinese-simplified.
